--- SOURCE 1: Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) ---
URL: https://icmra.info/drupal/en/strategicinitatives/amr

SUMMARY:
World Antimicrobial Awareness Week aims to raise awareness of the growing public health threat of antimicrobial resistance and to promote best practices

FULL CONTENT:
![Home](/drupal/sites/default/files/ICMRA%20logo_standard_0.png)

![ICMRA](/drupal/sites/default/files/inline-images/icmra_header_01.jpg)

![ICMRA](/drupal/sites/default/files/inline-images/icmra_header_01.jpg)

## Main navigation

# Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR)

### Global regulators commit to playing their part in tackling antimicrobial resistance as a priority

In this statement, ICMRA members reiterate that preserving the effectiveness of antimicrobials is a top priority globally for the protection of public health and that regulators have an important role to play. To tackle antimicrobial resistance, global regulators agree on the need for a multifaceted approach, which includes:

International regulators also stress their commitment to keep collaborating and joining forces to address the global public health challenge of antimicrobial resistance.

[ICMRA statement](/drupal/strategicinitatives/amr/icmra_statement_21nov2024)

**Other languages**

### World AMR Awareness Week 2024: Preventing antimicrobial resistance together

To mark World Antimicrobial Awareness Week in 2024, ICMRA has developed visual materials to raise awareness of the growing public health threat of antimicrobial resistance (AMR) including key figures and a call to action for global health leaders, decision-makers and the pharmaceutical industry to help stop this “silent pandemic”. The materials focus on the need for research and development of innovative antimicrobials and vaccines and diagnostic products that facilitate prudent and appropriate use.

ICMRA encourages stakeholders to join the fight against AMR and share the recommendations with others.

[Key messages](/drupal/sites/default/files/2024-11/waaw2024_keymessages.pdf)

### World AMR Awareness Week 2023: Preventing antimicrobial resistance together

In support of the World AMR Awareness Week on 18–24 November in 2023, ICMRA has developed key messages and visual materials to raise awareness of the growing public health threat of antimicrobial resistance (AMR) and to highlight successful regulatory and non-regulatory interventions. The visuals highlight actions and activities that can be taken to help combat AMR based on best practices from around the world.

ICMRA encourages stakeholders to join the fight against AMR and share the recommendations with others.

[Key messages](/drupal/sites/default/files/2023-11/waaw2023_keymessages.pdf)

### ICMRA AMR Best Practices Report

Building on the ICMRA’s antimicrobial resistance (AMR) joint [statement](/drupal/sites/default/files/2019-11/ICMRA_Antimicrobial_Resistance.pdf) published in 2019, the ICMRA adopted a new AMR project in 2021 on AMR best practices. The project leveraged the collective knowledge and experience of ICMRA members to develop case studies to highlight successful or promising regulatory and non-regulatory interventions in different jurisdictions to address AMR.

The ICMRA AMR Best Practices Report presents a compilation of case studies intended to serve as tools to raise awareness of important actions taken to date to combat AMR, within the ICMRA membership and beyond. These case studies also serve to promote further discussions within the ICMRA membership and opportunities to adapt these best practices for implementation, strengthening our resolve to take bold steps to address this important public health issue and setting the stage for future collaborations.

We all have a role to play in combatting AMR. ICMRA recognizes that AMR is a complex, multi-faceted problem, and is calling for a coordinated, One Health response across all sectors, including public health, animal health, and the environment. A concerted and collaborative effort from all partners is essential to ensuring our success in addressing this threat to our health, economies and security.

#### [Antimicrobial Resistance Best Practices - Working Group Report and Case Studies (November 2022)](/drupal/sites/default/files/2022-11/amr_best_practices_report.pdf)

**Other languages**

### World Antimicrobial Awareness Week 2022: Preventing antimicrobial resistance together

In support of World Antimicrobial Awareness Week in 2022, ICMRA has developed a set of info-cards to raise awareness of the growing public health threat of antimicrobial resistance (AMR) and to highlight successful regulatory and non-regulatory interventions. The info-cards highlight actions and activities stakeholders can take to help combat AMR based on best practices from around the world.

ICMRA encourages stakeholders to join the fight against AMR and share the recommendations with others.

[Info-cards](/drupal/sites/default/files/2022-11/waaw_2022_infocards_icmra.pdf)

### Global regulators support World Antimicrobial Awareness Week 2021

To mark World Antimicrobial Awareness Week in 2021, ICMRA has developed a set of info-cards to raise awareness of the growing public health problem of antimicrobial resistance (AMR) and the need to address it. The info-cards call on global health leaders, healthcare professionals and patients to take action.

ICMRA encourages stakeholders to join the fight against AMR and share the recommendations with others.

The latest set of info-cards builds on the success of the campaign launched during World Antimicrobial Awareness Week in 2020.

[Info-cards](/drupal/sites/default/files/2021-11/WAAW_2021_infocards_ICMRA.pdf)

**Other languages**

### Global regulators support World Antimicrobial Awareness Week 2020

In support of the World Antimicrobial Awareness Week 2020, which is marked from 18 to 24 November 2020, the International Coalition of Medicines Regulatory Authorities (ICMRA) has published a set of [info-cards](/drupal/sites/default/files/2020-11/WAAW_2020_infocards_ICMRA.pdf). The info-cards stress the need for cross-sectoral, global cooperation to combat antimicrobial resistance around the world and highlight the important role of global health leaders, medicines regulators, researchers, pharmaceutical industry and media.  
World Antimicrobial Awareness Week aims to raise awareness of the growing public health threat of antimicrobial resistance and to promote best practices among policy makers, healthcare professionals and the general public to avoid the emergence and spread of multidrug-resistant bacterial infections.  
‘The problem of antimicrobial resistance is multifaceted and cannot be addressed by one single organisation or sector. A coordinated global approach and commitment are required to tackle this public health threat,’ said Emer Cooke, Chair of ICMRA and EMA’s Executive Director. ’As global regulators, we have a responsibility to bring to the market safe, effective and high-quality medicines that can help combat antimicrobial resistance. International collaboration is crucial to streamline regulatory requirements and facilitate access to critical antimicrobials around the world.’  
Since 2019, ICMRA has convened several panel discussions with international regulators and other key stakeholders to provide strategic direction and find innovative solutions to fight the spread of antimicrobial resistance. As a result, ICMRA published a joint [statement](http://www.icmra.info/drupal/sites/default/files/2019-11/ICMRA_Antimicrobial_Resistance.pdf) on antimicrobial resistance aimed to reinforce national commitment and stimulate global action to address this public health threat. The statement is available in multiple languages.

### Antimicrobial Resistance Statement

[Antimicrobial Resistance Statement (July 2019)](http://www.icmra.info/drupal/sites/default/files/2019-11/ICMRA_Antimicrobial_Resistance.pdf)

--- SOURCE 2: World Antimicrobial Resistance Awareness Week 2024 - PAHO/WHO ---
URL: https://www.paho.org/en/campaigns/world-antimicrobial-resistance-awareness-week-2024

SUMMARY:
The World AMR Awareness Week (WAAW) is a global campaign to raise awareness and understanding of AMR and promote best practices among One Health stakeholders to reduce the emergence and spread of drug-resistant infections. WAAW is celebrated from 18-24 November every year.

FULL CONTENT:
## User account menu

![Pan American Health Organization](/themes/paho/images/logo-en.png)

# World Antimicrobial Resistance Awareness Week 2024

**18 - 24 November 2024**

# **Educate. Advocate. Act now.**

**#WAAW #AMR**

The World AMR Awareness Week (WAAW) is a global campaign to raise awareness and understanding of AMR and promote best practices among One Health stakeholders to reduce the emergence and spread of drug-resistant infections. WAAW is celebrated from 18-24 November every year.

[Antimicrobial resistance](/en/topics/antimicrobial-resistance "Antimicrobial Resistance") (AMR) is an increasingly serious threat to global public health that requires action across all government sectors and society. It has significant impacts on human and animal health, food production and the environment. Drug-resistant-pathogens pose a threat to everyone, everywhere. Yet, much more can be done to raise public and stakeholder awareness.

The theme for the WAAW this year is “**Educate. Advocate. Act now**.” It’s a call on the global community to educate stakeholders on AMR, advocate for bold commitments and take concrete actions in response to AMR. The [2024 UNGA High-level Meeting on AMR](/en/general-assembly-high-level-meeting-antimicrobial-resistance-2024 "UN General Assembly High-Level Meeting on antimicrobial resistance 2024") provides an important opportunity for world leaders to collectively address the looming threat AMR poses to global health, food security, and achieving the 2030 Sustainable Development Goals. Stronger political leadership, advocacy and accountability are needed at all levels to fight AMR and the time to act is now.

![](/sites/default/files/styles/max_1500x1500/public/hero/2024-10/antimicrobial-resistance-1500x93x.jpg?itok=vQi2GMur)

# World Antimicrobial Resistance Awareness Week 2024 (WAAW)

## At a glance

## Get involved with WAAW activities

From November 18 - November 24, TATFAR joins [World Antimicrobial Resistance Awareness Week (WAAW)](https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-amr-awareness-week/2024). WAAW is a global observance to raise awareness and increase understanding of [antimicrobial resistance](https://www.cdc.gov/antimicrobial-resistance/index.html) (AMR or AR). The theme of WAAW 2024 is "Educate. Advocate. Act now."

We can all take action to protect these important drugs so that they remain effective for people and animals—using the right antibiotic or antifungal, at the right time, dose and duration.

### TATFAR activities

### Canada

The Public Health Agency of Canada, Health Canada, Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada and the Canadian Food Inspection Agency are responding to antimicrobial resistance through actions outlined in the [Pan-Canadian Action Plan on AMR](https://www.canada.ca/en/public-health/services/publications/drugs-health-products/pan-canadian-action-plan-antimicrobial-resistance.html). For World AMR Awareness Week, we will be highlighting actions taken, and sharing resources and materials to raise awareness on antimicrobial resistance.

To mark World AMR Awareness Week, several Canadian federal department buildings will be lit blue to increase awareness of AMR. Participate in the World Health Organization's "[Go Blue For AMR"](https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.who.int%2Fcampaigns%2Fworld-amr-awareness-week%2F2023%2Fgo-blue-campaign&data=05%7C02%7Cema.rubignoni%40AGR.GC.CA%7C234eae1aadfa4085bcfc08dd05b883e1%7C9da98bb118574cc387519a49e35d24cd%7C0%7C0%7C638673012257145745%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=uPzE616zNyaGN4xhaV42IYdozJZ5m3Y%2FZdgXDz%2FfoiA%3D&reserved=0) [campaign](https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.who.int%2Fcampaigns%2Fworld-amr-awareness-week%2F2023%2Fgo-blue-campaign&data=05%7C02%7Cema.rubignoni%40AGR.GC.CA%7C234eae1aadfa4085bcfc08dd05b883e1%7C9da98bb118574cc387519a49e35d24cd%7C0%7C0%7C638673012257168151%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=RDY3X8g5TpMuBi0SM7cIC%2BWzAI2QNaFm9C1E9iI3jg4%3D&reserved=0), by:

#### Social Media

Keep an eye on the social channels below for updates during World AMR Awareness Week:

#### Resources and Materials

### European Union (EU)

[European Antibiotic Awareness Day](https://antibiotic.ecdc.europa.eu/en) (EAAD) takes place November 18 and is an annual European health initiative, coordinated by the European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control (ECDC) in partnership with WAAW. The latest data confirm that, across the European Union, the number of patients with infections caused by resistant germs is increasing and that antimicrobial resistance is a major threat to public health. You, too, can participate in EAAD!

### Norway

Norway's support of World Antimicrobial Resistance Awareness Week includes activities organized by various national competence centers, covering both primary and specialist healthcare services. At the national level, we aim to update our AMR data, and publish a news article on our website.

### United Kingdom (U.K.)

[England's national toolkit for WAAW and EAAD](https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/european-antibiotic-awareness-day-resources-toolkit-for-healthcare-professionals-in-england/world-antimicrobial-awareness-week-waaw-and-european-antibiotic-awareness-day-eaad) was launched in September and it details our key focus areas.

#### Themes

Daily themes have been mapped to highlight which of the 2024 - 2029 nation action plan they align to. [Resources for the daily themes](https://khub.net/web/ukhsa-english-surveillance-programme-for-antimicrobial-utilisation-and-resistance-espaur/public-library/-/document_library/9f5dxUnHMbJR/view/1032260782?_com_liferay_document_library_web_portlet_DLPortlet_INSTANCE_9f5dxUnHMbJR_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fkhub.net%3A443%2Fweb%2Fukhsa-english-surveillance-programme-for-antimicrobial-utilisation-and-resistance-espaur%2Fpublic-library%2F-%2Fdocument_library%2F9f5dxUnHMbJR%2Fview%2F1029570962%3F_com_liferay_document_library_web_portlet_DLPortlet_INSTANCE_9f5dxUnHMbJR_redirect%3Dhttps%253A%252F%252Fkhub.net%253A443%252Fweb%252Fukhsa-english-surveillance-programme-for-antimicrobial-utilisation-and-resistance-espaur%252Fpublic-library%253Fp_p_id%253Dcom_liferay_document_library_web_portlet_DLPortlet_INSTANCE_9f5dxUnHMbJR%2526p_p_lifecycle%253D0%2526p_p_state%253Dnormal%2526p_p_mode%253Dview).

There are 3 cross-cutting themes underpinning all 5 days:

[A summary of key messages](https://khub.net/documents/1028143313/1032312555/Summary+of+key+messages+2023+-+World+Antimicrobial+Awareness+Week.pdf/2555a47c-6130-c856-b8c5-7c0b63bf706f?t=1717081882868), aligned with each daily theme, can be downloaded to use for communications.

Social media hashtags: #ConfrontAMR, #AntibioticGuardian, #KeepAntibioticsWorking #WAAW

#### Key resources

### United States (U.S.)

U.S. Antibiotic Awareness Week (USAAW) is an annual one-week observance that raises awareness about the importance of appropriate use of antibiotics and antifungals and the threat antimicrobial resistance poses to people, animals, plants and their shared environment. U.S. Federal agencies, including the Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority (BARDA), the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID), and the Centers for Diseases Control and Prevention (CDC) will participate in the week using Facebook, X (formerly Twitter), Instagram and LinkedIn.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Global campaigns like World AMR Awareness Week engage communities, highlight AMR risks, and promote responsible antimicrobial use, fostering a collective response against this pressing health challenge.
Systematic tracking of antibiotic prescriptions helps identify misuse, guiding interventions to optimize usage and reduce the emergence of resistant strains among bacterial populations.
A One Health approach fosters collaboration between public health, animal health, and environmental sectors, ensuring comprehensive strategies to combat AMR on multiple fronts effectively.
Involving local communities in AMR discussions enhances understanding, encourages responsible behavior, and empowers individuals to participate actively in prevention efforts.

Educational initiatives focus on disseminating knowledge about AMR, promoting best practices in healthcare, and fostering a culture of antibiotic stewardship among healthcare professionals and the public.
